title: embeddings: route openai-3-small/large to direct OpenAI API
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
The Azure S0 deployment in East US has been the sole source of `openai-3-large` errors. In the last 24h: 479 of 502 errors (14% of total traffic) were Azure rate-limit messages (`exceeded the call rate limit for your current AIServices S0 pricing tier`) — surfaced to users as 502s by the upstream-429→502 remap.

Direct OpenAI has far higher rate-limit headroom and matches the existing per-token cost ($0.13/M for `text-embedding-3-large`, $0.02/M for `text-embedding-3-small`), so no registry pricing change.

- Adds `api.openai.com/v1/embeddings` client mirroring the prior Azure client (Bearer auth via `OPENAI_API_KEY`, already a worker binding used by `gpt-image-2`).
- Removes the Azure embeddings client — no other embedding model used it. `AZURE_MYCELI_PROD_API_KEY` is still consumed by text models, so the env var stays.
- Updates `provider: "azure"` → `"openai"` for both `openai-3-small` and `openai-3-large`.
- Updates the existing test suite to mock `api.openai.com` instead of the Azure host. All 16 embedding tests pass.

gist.headline: Unblocking the embeddings
gist.blurb: We've re-routed our openai-3-small and large embedding models directly to OpenAI to bypass some pesky Azure rate limits. Expect much smoother sailing and fewer 502 errors when generating embeddings!
gist.summary: Switched the provider for openai-3-small and openai-3-large embeddings from Azure to the direct OpenAI API to resolve frequent 502 rate-limit errors.
gist.impact: Users generating embeddings will experience significantly improved reliability and fewer failed requests, as the direct API offers much higher rate-limit headroom.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee acting as a network engineer, connecting a new, glowing data pipe. Nomnom eating the old 502 error logs.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee wearing a tiny yellow hard hat, happily connecting a glowing lime green (#ecf874) data pipe labeled 'OPENAI'. The round tan Nomnom creature sits nearby, enthusiastically eating a pile of red '502 ERROR' papers. Soft pastel gradient background with warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic, emotionally warm, and chunky retro sprites.
